articles.title: Americans Are Stuck in a Rut — And a Lack of Fun Might Be to Blame
articles.summary: America appears to be suffering from a fun shortage. From the rising cost of concert tickets, to bowling alley and nightclub closures, to venue shortages for kids to play in local sports leagues, the fun deficit is real and it's making Americans more isolated and lonelier than previous generations. Bloomberg's Ben Steverman joins Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin on the Everybody's Business podcast to explore some easy solutions to bring fun back into our everyday lives. (Source: Bloomberg)

Shipping data showed that traffic through the waterway has dropped sharply during the latest military attacks due to increased risk for ships crossing the strait, which supplies a fifth of the world's oil and gas supplies. articles.title: Israeli army says it killed Hamas commander in Gaza strike
articles.summary: Israeli army says it killed Hamas commander in Gaza strike Israel’s military said it killed a Hamas commander in a strike on southern Gaza on Thursday. In a statement, the army said the attack targeted Yahya Saeed Muhammad Hamdan, head of the Qassam Brigades Nukhba Forces unit, who allegedly took part in the 7 October 2023 attacks on Israel. There has been no immediate statement from Hamas, which recently said that its spokesperson Hazem Qassem survived an Israeli attack on Thursday west of Gaza City.

articles.title: Greece cuts fuel prices as Iran war strains household budgets
articles.summary: Greece cuts fuel prices as Iran war strains household budgets Greece has announced temporary cuts to petrol and diesel prices as the US-Israeli war on Iran drives up costs across Europe. Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis told parliament on Friday that the country’s two main oil refineries had agreed to reduce prices until the end of August. “Fuel prices will drop by 10 cents per litre and diesel fuel will drop by 5 cents per litre,” he said. The move follows growing pressure on household budgets as the war disrupts energy markets and pushes up transport and living costs across the continent.
